[2020-04-18 19:42] velizar_ (Name): Most Prismata games are won or lost in the first few turns. (Except for Turn 1). That is, for humans. But I assume for AI it will be similar.
[2020-04-18 19:43] sano712 (sano): eh I will object that statement about prismata having lots of trivially pointless moves
[2020-04-18 19:43] sano712 (sano): stuff like holding drones, splitters etc can be correct against threat, chill etc
[2020-04-18 19:43] sano712 (sano): so you can't just remove those from the state spac
[2020-04-18 19:44] sano712 (sano): i mean, we've seen games being won by floating 20 gold into buying 5 animus for 10 tarsiers
[2020-04-18 19:44] sano712 (sano): so even seemingly absurd plays can be correct
[2020-04-18 19:47] velizar_ (Name): I'm not ruling out absurd plays; I'm only ruling out plays which are strictly worse than a different play which is available to you.
[2020-04-18 19:48] velizar_ (Name): Even floating gold is done so that the gold can be used after 1 or 2 turns.
[2020-04-18 19:48] velizar_ (Name): It isn't done in situations where you don't have a good use for it in 1-2 turns.
[2020-04-18 19:48] siepu: simple exercise, try to count how many nonabsurd plays you can make on like turn 5 with some rescources already
[2020-04-18 19:49] velizar_ (Name): That prunes the search-space quite a bit
[2020-04-18 19:49] siepu: I think you will get to 50
[2020-04-18 19:49] siepu: and then it only gets more complicated
[2020-04-18 19:49] siepu: and if you want to solve the set you have to check every path
[2020-04-18 19:49] siepu: its really fucking hard
[2020-04-18 19:50] willflame: it's not that there aren't a lot of viable paths, it's just that there are even more possible paths in total and many of them are strictly worse than the viable ones
[2020-04-18 19:51] siepu: I excluded the paths that you can prove are worse (holding drones below the absorb barrier)
[2020-04-18 19:52] siepu: but you cant easily prove floating is strictly worse
[2020-04-18 19:52] lyra1712 (Lyra): 50 is a ridiculous underestimation
[2020-04-18 19:52] velizar_ (Name): Chess is much worse, we aren't even 100% sure that in an optimal play, black doesn't win. 
As a chess player, you can always do the same move in your first turn, based on the statistics of how often it wins - you don't need to think about it. But you can't rule out absurd plays for first move, if you're considering EVERYTHING.
Likewise, as a chess player, you use heuristics such as "tempo is good" and "having more figures is good", but there will be rare counter-intuitive exceptions to those. If you're considering everything, you're in trouble.
[2020-04-18 19:53] siepu: chess has a simplie list of all possible moves every turn
[2020-04-18 19:54] siepu: even if an average game is longer than in prismata
[2020-04-18 19:55] velizar_ (Name): My point is that simple brute-forcing Chess would be much easier than simple brute-forcing Prismata; but if instead you're doing brute-forcing while only considering moves which aren't strictly inferior to another move, then Chess should be harder.
[2020-04-18 19:55] velizar_ (Name): And in my opinion, Prismata with a fixed extra set should be easier to write a good AI for.
[2020-04-18 19:55] lyra1712 (Lyra): i agree with your first point, disagree about the second
[2020-04-18 19:56] siepu: depends what a good ai is
[2020-04-18 19:56] siepu: i think master bot is good ai
[2020-04-18 19:56] velizar_ (Name): stronger than most players.
[2020-04-18 19:56] lyra1712 (Lyra): even if you disregard strictly dominated moves, you end up with a much bigger game tree in prismata
[2020-04-18 19:56] velizar_ (Name): That's _only_ if you disregard strictly dominated moves?
[2020-04-18 19:57] lyra1712 (Lyra): no, obviously if you dont the game tree is even bigger
[2020-04-18 19:57] velizar_ (Name): Oh yeah you're right
[2020-04-18 19:57] velizar_ (Name): I misunderstood what  "disregard" applies to
[2020-04-18 19:58] velizar_ (Name): I disagree though. Prismata tends to have fewer turns, and fewer non-dominated options per turn, right?
[2020-04-18 19:58] lyra1712 (Lyra): fewer turns sure, fewer non-dominated option i disagree
[2020-04-18 19:59] lyra1712 (Lyra): suppose you have 15 drones, how many different things can you buy (including floating gold), using only base set
[2020-04-18 19:59] lyra1712 (Lyra): i dont see at first glance why any of these option could be strictly dominated
[2020-04-18 20:00] velizar_ (Name): I guess that optimal defense is ... NP-hard, but usually Q-defense is the optimal.
[2020-04-18 20:00] lyra1712 (Lyra): maybe also suppose you have a blastforge and an animus
[2020-04-18 20:00] lyra1712 (Lyra): then you'll literally have thousands of options
[2020-04-18 20:00] lyra1712 (Lyra): none of them would be strictly dominated
[2020-04-18 20:01] lyra1712 (Lyra): obviously most would be stupid
[2020-04-18 20:01] velizar_ (Name): Let me open a random replay at a random turn
[2020-04-18 20:03] lyra1712 (Lyra): defense is way easier than buy phase
[2020-04-18 20:05] velizar_ (Name): Yeah I don't think I can convince myself that not getting breached tends to be provably strictly worse than getting breached.
[2020-04-18 20:05] velizar_ (Name): So buying steelsplitters instead of walls will be a consideration.
[2020-04-18 20:06] velizar_ (Name): Alright fair enough, you've convinced me.
[2020-04-18 20:06] velizar_ (Name): If anyone wants to see an arbitrary game state, here's what I looked at
[2020-04-18 20:07] velizar_ (Name): There's only one way to defend, thanks to Shaiev which says that you never prefer to have fewer units rather than more
[2020-04-18 20:07] lyra1712 (Lyra): yeah, defense is mostly easy, or at least has few non-dominated options

[2020-04-19 11:31] flakmaniak: Ok so WTF is going on here, and WTF should have been going on: 8TC1i-KzsZI
[2020-04-19 11:31] flakmaniak: I was sooo confused the whole game, about what I should be doing.
[2020-04-19 11:31] flakmaniak: When the guy built double Blastforge, I wasn't thinking about Grenade Mech at all; I assumed immediate Deadeye was the play.
[2020-04-19 11:31] flakmaniak: So I wasn't ready to defend.
[2020-04-19 11:31] flakmaniak: Getting breached seemed the least bad option there.
[2020-04-19 11:32] flakmaniak: Killing my Animus seemed correct; meant I had to wait ages to build more Scorches.
[2020-04-19 11:32] flakmaniak: Neither player going for triple Engie seemed right, given there aren't any good absorbers. Well, Mega Drone, I guess. Not that you'd want to use it that way.
[2020-04-19 11:33] flakmaniak: I figured that as P2, it's an easy DD/DDC into Scorches, but as you can see, it wasn't that simple.
[2020-04-19 11:33] flakmaniak: But... Man, what a weird set, and game.
[2020-04-19 11:33] flakmaniak: Some REALLY interesting midgame positions.
[2020-04-19 11:34] flakmaniak: That one wouldn't get in a normal, non-awkward game.
[2020-04-19 11:39] velizar_ (Name): Your opponent should have won as soon as you didn't react to the double blastforge. They weren't supposed to build a deadeye, they were supposed to build a second gmech, and also kill drones instead of your animus. Then you'd be left with
[2020-04-19 11:40] velizar_ (Name): Not noticing G Mech in the set was a game losing mistake
[2020-04-19 11:42] velizar_ (Name): Then you'll be in this state:
[2020-04-19 11:43] velizar_ (Name): Actually this doesn't seem like an obviously losing state...
[2020-04-19 11:44] velizar_ (Name): Anyway, after that, your opponent made more mistakes:
[2020-04-19 11:45] velizar_ (Name): Holding 6 drones and a steelsplitter was a mistake - they should have let you breach. They ended up losing 3 drones and a husk.
[2020-04-19 11:46] velizar_ (Name): If they instead clicked the splitter and all drones and floated the 6 gold, then they would have lost 4 drones.
[2020-04-19 11:46] velizar_ (Name): This mistake costed XQ10XA 1 attack and ~5 gold. Really big mistake.
[2020-04-19 11:48] velizar_ (Name): Fun fact: losing a clicked drone is just as bad as losing an engineer. If your most valuable unit for breaching is a drone, and you're not going to get any absorb, then losing clicked drones isn't actually bad. The bad thing about getting breached for drones is losing the ability to absorb.
[2020-04-19 11:52] velizar_ (Name): If you have infinite energy, and have two options:
1. buy some engis
2. buy the same amount of drones
then the units you bought are going to get killed as soon as they are built, then in case 2 you still gain back one gold from clicking the drones, so the drones end up costing you 2 gold each.
[2020-04-19 11:57] flakmaniak: Sacrificing all the Blastforges so aggressively seemed weird as well; the guy kind of ran out of steam (and persistent attack) by doing that. And the Chrono Filter didn't really seem like enough.
[2020-04-19 11:57] flakmaniak: When the guy built the first Grenade Mech, do you think it was correct for me to just allow the breach? Blocking just didn't seem very good there.
[2020-04-19 11:58] flakmaniak: So I figured, whatever, gonna set up my Scorches, and if he kills my Animus, then I'll figure out what to do about that later.
[2020-04-19 11:59] velizar_ (Name): I might have bought rhino+forcefield in order to get absorb. Hmm...
[2020-04-19 12:00] velizar_ (Name): I'm not sure if rhino+forcefield is better than allowing breach.
[2020-04-19 12:04] flakmaniak: I figured a breach wasn't all that bad at that point, and I really wanted to get some attackers set up, to not be on the back foot forever.
[2020-04-19 12:04] velizar_ (Name): Ok I was wrong about clicked drones being as good defense as engis. Actually engis are more cost-effective. Although my overall point remains valid that XQ10XA shouldn't have held the drones & splitter.
[2020-04-19 12:07] velizar_ (Name): Yeah I'm not sure either.

[2020-04-20 17:24] stacko: Sorry for the vague answer. For those flame animus openings, the main thing I would consider is if it's a high econ set or not. If not, and there's a pretty good red unit like grimbotch or something, that turn 2 blastforge -> turn 3 flame animus seems good. If there's something like energy matrix in the set, you'd definitely want to get a decent bit of economy before starting to buy attackers, so the other line would be better. Obviously, consider what your opponent is doing, in case you need to pivot your build to counter them.

Thorium is almost a must-buy in most sets. The guideline is to try and not waste energy or gold when building your economy, and thorium lets you spend a bit more gold despite not having more energy. Having some green is never a problem, since it's the most flexible resource, especially if you can turn it back into gold. The only time I would consider skipping thorium entirely is if it opens me up to a red rush that I wouldn't be able to defend against.

[2020-04-22 22:08] zera777 (Zera): How would you play this set?
[2020-04-22 22:09] velizar_ (Name): I have my eye on the Cynestra. It's the most cost-effective attacker after Shadowfang and the 4-attack 17RRRR ant girl.
[2020-04-22 22:10] velizar_ (Name): Also you simultaneously threaten Tia, which forces your opponent into preparing defense which they won't need. So they'll probably have to buy a blastforge.
[2020-04-22 22:11] velizar_ (Name): Bombarder is also strong tho.
[2020-04-22 22:11] velizar_ (Name): Oooh, in fact going bombarder on low economy might be great.
[2020-04-22 22:12] velizar_ (Name): on consecutive turns do those:
1. animus
2. smorcus
3. click smorcus, buy double blastforge
[2020-04-22 22:12] velizar_ (Name): since Cynestra needs a much higher economy (for walls)
[2020-04-22 22:13] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): that's one juice set. my gameplan would be:

1. play the set and lose
2. play it again an lose
3. play it again an lose
4. play ti again an lose
5. ask me again
[2020-04-22 22:14] velizar_ (Name): I wonder if Thunderhead would also screw up the Cynestra. Since the 4 attack per turn hurts!
[2020-04-22 22:15] velizar_ (Name): There are many plays.

[2020-04-22 23:32] flakmaniak: What're some good Smorcus openings, anyway? I never got a good read on that unit. Sometimes I buy it when I don't have much gold and just want a persistent attacker. Sometimes it just fits in conveniently, when I don't have the gold for two Tarsiers or whatever. And it's clearly pretty good to be able to threaten it every turn, forcing them to overdefend constantly.
[2020-04-22 23:32] flakmaniak: But... I guess I'm not sure it's a crazy rush unit; usually I think I'd force a Wall with it and then just, I dunno, build Tarsiers or whatever.
[2020-04-22 23:34] flakmaniak: I mean, if I can't buy two Tarsiers because of gold limitations, then a single Smorcus is a Fast Tarsier with +1 threat. Which is nice!
[2020-04-22 23:34] flakmaniak: But I never feel like I'm basing my strategy on it.
[2020-04-22 23:35] flakmaniak: Once or twice I might have used a second Animus's output on one, but I got that second Animus for other reasons.

[2020-04-22 23:52] velizar_ (Name): Animus shines at early game, and it's the worst at lategame, where you're no longer buying attackers, just soak
[2020-04-22 23:53] velizar_ (Name): I buy Smorcus when I want animus early, but something else late
[2020-04-22 23:55] velizar_ (Name): It's also great if you want more than 1 animus. Like for a Lucina.
[2020-04-22 23:55] velizar_ (Name): Oh and it's good to get it even before you'll need to click it, because then it will provide 2 threat.
[2020-04-22 23:57] velizar_ (Name): Units like Wincer, Valkyrion, Colossus might leave you overteched, that's when smorc helps spend your red
